Title: “Candidatus Liberibacter asiaticus” Infection Induces Citric Acid Accumulation and Immune Responses Mediated by the Transcription Factor CitPH4

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SA treatment upregulated CsPH1 expression, consistent with activation of citric acid biosynthesis/acidification pathway.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsPH1 was induced in SA-treated citrus fruits. 2. The gene is part of the citric-acid-related acidification module responding to SA. 3. It was not the primary manipulative target of the study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because it is an experimentally measured non-enzyme/non-TF effector in the CA pathway; authors state CsPH1 contributes to fruit hyperacidification.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced in kumquat fruits after SA treatment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit acidity and, indirectly in this study, association with defence response crosstalk
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be co-targeted with CitPH4/CsPH5 for tuning fruit acidity; not established here as standalone breeding target.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher acidity may alter flavour balance.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SA treatment upregulated CsPH5 expression, consistent with activation of citric acid accumulation/fruit hyperacidification.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsPH5 expression increased after SA treatment. 2. CsPH5 is part of the vacuolar proton-pumping complex linked to citrus fruit hyperacidification. 3. It functions downstream of the CA–SA crosstalk described in the paper.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors explicitly note CsPH1 and CsPH5 encode subunits of a vacuolar proton-pumping P-ATPase complex contributing to hyperacidification.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced in kumquat fruits after SA treatment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit acidity and defense-associated metabolic state
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream marker/engineering partner for acidity tuning.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess acidification may affect eating quality.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: AvrPphB SUSCEPTIBLE3 / GH3 acyl-adenylase family protein
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a key step in the isochorismate-derived salicylic acid biosynthetic route.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point supported by this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated by CitPH4, leading to increased SA biosynthesis and accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsPBS3 expression was increased in CitPH4-overexpressing tobacco and CLas-infected citrus fruits. 2. CsPBS3 expression decreased in CitPH4-knockout citrus fruits. 3. CitPH4 directly bound and activated the CsPBS3 promoter, supporting CsPBS3 as a key SA biosynthetic gene in the CitPH4-mediated response.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; metabolite profiling of SA in OE/KO backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CLas infection; CitPH4 overexpression or knockout
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in CLas-infected fruits; induced in CitPH4-OE background
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate downstream target to enhance SA-mediated HLB tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated SA signaling may affect growth or defense-growth balance, not tested here.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome cited from PRJNA1076338; targeted metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although measured partly in heterologous tobacco, the direct promoter regulation evidence is from transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana with citrus promoter constructs.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression correlated positively with CitPH4 activity and CLas infection, consistent with increased SA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsICS1 expression was elevated in CitPH4-overexpressing plants and CLas-infected fruits. 2. CsICS1 expression was reduced in CitPH4-knockout citrus fruits. 3. Direct activation by CitPH4 was not demonstrated, unlike CsPBS3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase negative/less supported relative to CsPBS3; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CLas infection; CitPH4 overexpression or knockout
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in infected fruits and CitPH4-OE plants
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential partner gene for engineering stronger SA biosynthesis, though direct control by CitPH4 remains unclear.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely defense-growth tradeoff if constitutively activated; not tested.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome cited from PRJNA1076338; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes CsPBS3 more strongly as the direct CitPH4 target.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Citrus spp.; Nicotiana tabacum; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CsPBS3; CA-related genes including CsPH1 and CsPH5/acidification module (context from prior work and treatment responses)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates CsPBS3 promoter; positively associated with expression of SA biosynthesis/signaling genes CsICS1, CsCBP60G, CsPAD4, CsNPR1 and CsPR1.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: citric acid accumulation/fruit acidification and salicylic acid biosynthesis/signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Can act independently or synergistically with CitAN1 in transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Links reciprocal CA–SA positive interaction; exact formal feedback architecture not fully established
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key node connecting citric acid accumulation and salicylic acid-mediated immunity during HLB response
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes citric acid accumulation and salicylic acid biosynthesis; enhances tolerance to CLas when overexpressed.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitPH4 expression is upregulated in CLas-infected citrus fruits, coincident with increased citric acid and salicylic acid levels. 2. Overexpression of CitPH4 in tobacco increased citric acid and salicylic acid contents and reduced CLas titres after graft inoculation. 3. Knockout of CitPH4 in citrus fruits reduced expression of SA-pathway genes and lowered SA content. 4. CitPH4 directly binds and activates the CsPBS3 promoter, establishing a mechanistic link to SA biosynthesis. 5. CitPH4 therefore integrates fruit quality (acid accumulation) with defence response.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in tobacco; CRISPR/gene editing knockout in citrus; RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; CLas graft inoculation assay; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CLas infection; exogenous SA and CA treatments
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: citrus fruit vacuole for citric acid; infected tissues/fruits for salicylic acid
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: citric acid contributes to fruit acidity and defense-associated antimicrobial/immune state; salicylic acid mediates immune signaling against CLas
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising breeding/engineering target to couple improved HLB tolerance with controlled fruit acidity; expression fine-tuning is recommended rather than simple constitutive overexpression.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter fruit flavor/acidity balance; broader growth penalties were not reported.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome accession PRJNA1076338; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors emphasize pleiotropy and future value for breeding high-quality, disease-resistant citrus.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Mashui mandarin; Shatang mandarin; Pokan mandarin; kumquat; tobacco; Arabidopsis

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CsPBS3 promoter in combination with CitPH4
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Synergistically enhances CitPH4-mediated activation of CsPBS3.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms transcription activation complex with CitPH4
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive co-activator, not the main decisive regulator in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances activation of CsPBS3 together with CitPH4 in transient assays.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitAN1 can synergize with CitPH4 to activate CsPBS3 promoter activity. 2. It is presented as a cofactor rather than the main subject regulating SA accumulation in the study. 3. No independent genetic validation on metabolite levels was shown.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible combinatorial engineering partner with CitPH4, but evidence here is limited.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally tested in Results, though its role is secondary/background relative to CitPH4.
